description:
You are a cloud-architect who is migrating an internal micro-service from the development cluster to the production cluster.  
    The existing development configuration is already present on disk at  

        /home/user/legacy_configs/payment_service.conf  

    Your job is to produce a production-ready configuration **and** to leave behind a short, machine-readable migration log.

    1. Create a new directory called  
           /home/user/prod_configs  
       (create any parent folders that do not yet exist).

    2. Inside that directory create a file named  
           /home/user/prod_configs/payment_service.conf  

       The file **must contain exactly the five lines below and in the given order**:

           SERVICE_NAME=payment
           SERVICE_PORT=9090
           ENVIRONMENT=production
           DB_HOST=localhost
           DB_PORT=5432

       • Do **not** add extra spaces, blank lines, or comments.  
       • Make sure the old port number (8080) is fully replaced with 9090.  
       • Make sure the new key ENVIRONMENT is inserted exactly as shown.

    3. Create (or overwrite, if it already exists) the log file  

           /home/user/migration_logs/payment_service_migration.log  

       with exactly these four lines and in this order:

           TIMESTAMP: MIGRATION_COMPLETED
           Old_Port=8080
           New_Port=9090
           ENVIRONMENT_SET=production

       • Do **not** include additional text, blank lines, or a trailing newline after the last line.

    Automated tests will verify:

       a) The directory /home/user/prod_configs exists.  
       b) The file /home/user/prod_configs/payment_service.conf exists with **exactly** the five required lines, correct spelling, ordering, and values.  
       c) The log file /home/user/migration_logs/payment_service_migration.log exists with **exactly** the four required lines, correct spelling, ordering, and values.  

    Hints:

       • You only need a handful (≈2-3) simple shell commands to copy the old file, edit the port and add the ENVIRONMENT line, and then write the log.  
       • Because no root privileges are available, stay strictly inside /home/user.  
       • Use whatever text-processing tool (sed, awk, printf, etc.) you prefer, as long as the final contents match the specification **exactly**.

    When all three items above are satisfied, the migration is considered complete.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts:

    • The directory /home/user/legacy_configs already exists.  
    • /home/user/legacy_configs/payment_service.conf contains exactly:

          SERVICE_NAME=payment
          SERVICE_PORT=8080
          DB_HOST=localhost
          DB_PORT=5432

    • The directories /home/user/prod_configs and /home/user/migration_logs do NOT exist.

    Expected final state after correct task execution:

    1. /home/user/prod_configs exists (0700 or 0755 permissions accepted).  

    2. /home/user/prod_configs/payment_service.conf exists and contains **exactly**:

          SERVICE_NAME=payment
          SERVICE_PORT=9090
          ENVIRONMENT=production
          DB_HOST=localhost
          DB_PORT=5432

       (five newline-terminated lines, no extra whitespace; the terminating newline after the final line is optional and will not be tested).

    3. /home/user/migration_logs directory exists (0700 or 0755 accepted).

    4. /home/user/migration_logs/payment_service_migration.log exists and contains **exactly**:

          TIMESTAMP: MIGRATION_COMPLETED
          Old_Port=8080
          New_Port=9090
          ENVIRONMENT_SET=production

       (four newline-terminated lines, no extra whitespace; the terminating newline after the final line is optional and will not be tested).
